name: ibm_spectrum_lsf
explanation: IBM Spectrum LSF is a HPC workload scheduler. The check executes vendor CLI binaries (`lsid`, `lsclusters`, `lshosts`, `bhosts`, `lsload`, `bqueues`, `bjobs`, `bhist`, `bslots`, `badmin perfmon`, etc.) via `subprocess.run` and parses their output - it does not speak any network protocol. To run, the Agent host must (a) have the LSF client binaries installed and on PATH, (b) be configured as an LSF submission/management host with valid LSF environment (LSF_TOP, LSF_ENVDIR, profile.lsf sourced) so the binaries authenticate against the cluster master, and (c) supply the operator-chosen `cluster_name`. Pattern L (local CLI subprocess) plus an unknowable user-policy field (cluster name) makes this not autodiscoverable: the Agent cannot infer which LSF cluster name to attach without either reading LSF config files or being told.
notes: Borderline custom/other (presence of LSF binaries is host-local discoverable), but the required `cluster_name` field is operator policy and the LSF environment carries implicit credentials/host trust, so impossible is the more accurate classification.
